LEAGUE CALENDAR: Opening of the new season (Offseason Period 2/1-8/1)
Deadline to submit Tagged Players to League
Period for bidding on tagged players.
Deadline to match highest bid for tagged players.
Deadline to promote Developmental Squad players with (0) years left on their contract to their active roster.
Cap Penalty Cutoff date for current season
Players may officially be put on IR
Rosters must be cut down to Max of 26 Min of 23.
All Contracts must be finalized including Off-Season Extensions for players returning for final season.
Trading period ends at kickoff of the 1st game of the week.
Regular season ends. All acquired players after this point will be dropped at the conclusion of the Season (Week 16)
All IR players must either be dropped or activated to active roster
MEMBERSHIP: Established GMs may return from year to year provided their team remains in good standing.
Any new members must submit a request to the CC providing them with their fantasy football experience.
The CC will grant membership into the UPSFFL at their discretion based upon the request submitted by the prospective GM.
Preference will be given to GMs with prior dynasty experience, more than 3 years redraft experience, or new GMs that have been “vouched” for by existing GMs.
CHANGING OF MEMBERSHIP: In the event there is a change in membership, the new GM will keep the same players that were on the roster from the previous GM, as well as any draft positioning or draft picks belonging to the team in question
If more than 1 owner changes in the off-season we will hold a New Owner’s Only Draft.
Any Cap Penalties associated with the prior GM will NOT be transferred to the new GM.
GM RESPONSIBILITIES: GMs are expected to remain active and engaged at all times, including during the off-season. GMs should respect their fellow GMs and return emails, Private Messages, and trade offers in a timely manner. During the season this should be within 48-72 hours and in the off-season this should be within a week.
GM’s required to respond to trade requests within 96 hours during the season and within 2 weeks in the off-season.

An owner who fails to do so may be reported to a member of the CC.  This will result in 1 strike.  If an owner receives 3 strikes within the same season (Feb 1st –Jan 31st) they will be prohibited from making trades the remainder of that season.

GMs must attend or participate in ALL league-sponsored activities (calendar), or inform the commissioner ahead of time if they will not be able to attend.  If a previously scheduled event needs to be rescheduled, the CC will attempt to find a suitable time for all league members, with a goal being to complete activities in a timely and efficient manner.
LEAGUE RULES & VOTING: Major rule changes can only be made through a league-wide vote.
League off-season votes require 51% to pass.
The CC may make minor rule changes in the off-season only.
In some instances there may be more than one option.  If one of the options does not receive 7 votes, the two highest vote getters will then be polled among all 12 league owners for a final ruling.
In-season votes, though discouraged, require 90% to pass.

Votes will normally be conducted by e-mail or league poll.  Non-returned ballots are not factored in the returns.  Off-season votes are due within 7 days.  In-season votes are due within 48 hours of the time sent.

There are certain situations where some leniency is needed or a rule does not address an issue specifically.  In these situations, the Competition Committee will have the final say on the rule or rules in question.  A rule will then be put into action to address the concerned situation to prevent this from happening again in the future.
The divisions will stay the same way for 3 seasons.  After the 3rd season there will be realignment.  This will occur every 3 years thereafter.
Realignment will be based on Owner’s Historical “All-Play Record” (Regular Season)
The owner’s rank amongst league members will determine their division and conference membership.

Any new member coming in during a realignment year will automatically be assigned a 7 ranking adjusting other owner’s rankings accordingly.  If there is more than one owner we will work our way down from their adjusting other owner’s ranking accordingly.
STARTING LINEUP RULES: If a team owner submits a starting lineup with a player who is on a bye week, listed as OUT on the injury report, they will receive a warning.
If the player is listed as “Questionable” on the official NFL Injury Report within 24 hours of that player’s game and then becomes deactivated, this warning will not apply.
Note: If the player is listed as “Doubtful” on the official NFL Injury Report within 24 hours of that player’s game and then becomes deactivated, this warning will apply.
STARTING LINEUPS COMBINATIONS: Standard Lineup:  QB, 2RB, 3WR, TE, K, P, DE,LB,DB,D,D
Two backs, double TE:  QB, 2RB, 2WR, 2TE, K, P, DE,LB,DB,D,D
Single back, 1 TE:  QB, RB, 4WR, TE, K, P, DE,LB,DB,D,D
Single back, double TE:  QB, RB, 3WR, 2TE, K, P, DE,LB,DB,D,D
Three back:  QB, 3RB, 2WR, TE, K, P, DE,LB,DB,D,D
Three tight end:  QB, RB, 2WR, 3TE, K, P, DE,LB,DB,D,D
ROOKIES ONLY DRAFT: Beginning around the middle of July 2012, we will conduct a rookie’s only draft.  This process will occur annually and will consist of 5 rounds.
The time limit per pick is capped at 12 hours.  If a selection is not made within the 12 hour time frame the pick will be selected for owner using current ADP from www.mockdraftcentral.com.
Draft order is constant, like the NFL, and is determined by a team’s order of finish in the playoffs.
Any player eligible for the NFL rookie draft qualifies as an eligible player for the rookie draft.
If you have a question about a specific player’s eligibility please present the question to the league prior to the rookie draft.
Generally, any players who are eligible for the NFL draft or who is a NFL free agent with no prior experience in the league is considered eligible.
Players remaining in college for the following season are not eligible.
Players selected in the 1st round are not allowed to be on Developmental Roster.  They either must be on active roster, cut, or traded.
FREE AGENT DRAFT: Any player not on a roster following the Rookie Draft will be eligible for this draft.
Draft will be in proxy style format (EBay Style. Starting around early August)
The timer for each player is 24 hours
The timer resets if the high bidder changes
All bids will be in $1,000 increments.

Remember just like with EBAY, you can set a maximum bid amount but not necessarily spend that maximum amount.  (Ex. You want Tom Brady, you bid a maximum amount of $35,000 for him.  The previously high bid was $20,000.  The high bid is now yours at $21,000.  If another owner bids $27,000, you would still retain the high bid now at $28,000

Starting at 7:00 AM Day 1 all owners may nominate 1 player per day.  At 7:00 AM the next day an owner may nominate another player.  It is each owner’s responsibility to each nominate at least 1 player every 24 hour time frame until they have enough players on their roster or are the high bidder to have a complete team.
Teams must have a minimum of 23 players but NO MORE than 31 at the completion of the draft.
Owners will have until September 1st to submit their player’s contract length to the league via the league message board. Please see Contracts
Under NO scenario will an owner be allowed to retract a bid.  Make sure you are 100% comfortable with your bid ahead of time.

Teams may not bid on a player if it puts them over the salary cap or if they don’t have enough money remaining to fulfill the minimum roster requirements using the minimum bid for remaining open roster spots.
NEW OWNER\u2019S ONLY DRAFT: Should we have an off-season in which we need to replace more than 1 owner, we will conduct a “New Owner’s Only Draft” prior to the Rookies and the Free Agent draft.
All players and draft picks on the franchises taken over will be tossed into a pool.  This includes Developmental Players, and players whose contract may have just expired.
The draft will be conducted in a “Snake Draft” format.
Draft order will be decided by random draft generator from www.fftoolbox.com
Owners will draft players as well as draft picks until all players who are under contract from these teams is exhausted
Owners are not only drafting the player but the contract as well.
Any owner who takes over a team may make 1 cut without any cap penalty implications provided they announce they are using their cap exemption cut via the message board.
BASICS: Only players who are on the Active Roster, Injured Reserve (50%), Special Circumstance Players (25%), or Cap Penalties count against the salary cap.
During the off-season up until the Free-Agent  Draft the Salary Cap is not in effect.
BASIC CONTRACT LIFE: Contracts Roll-Forward on February 1st every year
If Contract = 3, then Contract goes to 2
If Contract = 2, then Contract goes to 1.
If Contract = 1, then owner has 3 options.

Only 1 extension per Player per Contract is allowed.  (If player goes back into Free Agent  and is won by same owner, this is a new contract and thus an extension may be given once contract goes to 0)
If Contract = 0, then owner has 3 options:
Franchise Tag – Minimum salary offer must be equal more or equal to the top 5 average of the position and must exceed player’s current contract.
Transition Tag – Minimum salary offer must be equal more or equal to the top 10 average of the position and must exceed player’s current contract.
No tag -- Player becomes Free Agent and must be dropped after the June 1st Deadline to designate Franchise and Transition players.
Franchises may not have more than six (Full 3 year Contracts)
Franchises may not have more than 5 Front or Back Loaded Contracts on their roster at any point.
OFF-SEASON EXTENSIONS: Extend the contract 1 year in addition to the upcoming season.  Contract year extends to 2 (two).
Must give the player a raise of a minimum based on chart below for each position or 20% of the player’s AVERAGE salary from current contract (whichever is greater)
AVERAGE = Total Salary/Length of Original Contract
The player’s raise is effective immediately and applies to both of the remaining years.
Extend the contract 2 years more years in addition to the upcoming season.  Contract year extends to 3 (three).
Must give the player a raise of a minimum based on chart below for each position or 40% of the player’s AVERAGE salary from current contract (whichever is greater)
AVERAGE = Total Salary/Length of Original Contract
The player’s raise is effective immediately and applies to all of the remaining years.
IN-SEASON EXTENSIONS: Players acquired via Waivers or via Blind Bid Free Agency
Player must be declared on League Message Board within 2 weeks of acquisition that is being extended.
Follows same rules as SECTION 6.03 OFF-SEASON EXTENSIONS with following exception:
The player’s raise goes into effect for the FOLLOWING SEASON.  However, if cut the total salary (Current + Future Raise is accounted for)
Only players that have 1 year remaining on contract or Franchise/Transition Tagged Players are eligible.
Player must be declared on League Message Board within 2 weeks of acquisition that is being extended.
ROOKIE CONTRACTS: Is a structured contract that applies only to players selected in the Rookie Only Draft.
All contracts are 2 years in length.
The salary of the first player taken in draft PICK 1.01 is $15,000.
Subsequent picks decrease $1,000 per player down to a minimum of $5,000 through the end of the second round.
Players selected in the 3rd round and beyond are $2,000
Once a player on developmental squad becomes activated, their salary counts against the cap.
After the Rookie contract has expired, owner’s may choose 1 of 3 options
Tag the Player as a Franchise or Transition Player
FRANCHISE AND TRANSITIONAL TAGS: Franchise and Transition players are named on June 1st.  At this time, the team owner must tag any potential free agents on their team, players with (0) zero remaining contract years, with a qualifying offer. All tags and bids must be posted to the league’s message board to be considered official.
Any potential free agents not tagged on or before June 1st become unrestricted free agents immediately and are eligible for the free agent .
Open bidding on tagged players begins on June 2nd and ends on June 15th via league message board.
Franchise Tag Specifics
Only 1 Player per team may be tagged with “Franchise Tag”
Minimum salary offer must be equal more or equal to the top 5 average of the position and must exceed player’s current contract.
During open bidding, a team may outbid the initial qualifying offer for the rights to this player.
If at the end of the franchise period, the qualifying offer IS NOT outbid, the original owner has 2 options.
They may retain that player’s rights for 1 year at the Qualifying offer price.  Player will become a free-agent the following season.
They may cut the player prior to the Free-Agent  Draft with no Cap Penalty.
If at the end of the franchise period, the qualifying offer IS outbid, the original owner has 2 options:
Match the highest offer made by the end of the day on June 17th.  This will allow owner to retain player for 1 year at the matched Salary
Allow the player to “walk” and receive compensation from the team that had the highest bid.
Compensation for a Franchised Tagged Player is Two (2) first round draft picks.
In most cases this will result in the current season as well as the following season’s pick to be relinquished to the owner who lost the player.

In the event the owner who won the bid on the Franchise Player has two (2) first round picks in the upcoming draft, the team that let the player “walk” can decide what picks they want to receive if the player that they let “walk” ended the prior season in the Top 5 in Total Points for their respective position.

In other words, Team A lets a player “walk” who was the 5th highest rated player (or better) in his position the previous season, to Team B who has the 1st pick overall as well as Pick 10.  In this scenario, Team A can choose both picks from this upcoming season or can choose 1 pick in current season and 1 pick the following season.
If Team A lets a player “walk” who was ranked 6th or lower, Team B decides what picks he will relinquish.
Unless a team has the required compensation necessary to compensate an owner, they will be prohibited from placing any such bid on a “Franchise Tagged Player”
If player is allowed to walk, the team that has won the “Franchise Tagged” player has the right to offer said a player a multiple year contract prior to Free-Agent  Draft.
Transition Tag Specifics
Only 1 Player per team may be tagged with “Transition Tag”
Minimum salary offer must be equal more or equal to the top 5 average of the position and must exceed player’s current contract.
During open bidding, a team may over bid the initial qualifying offer for the rights to this player.
If at the end of the franchise period, the qualifying offer is not outbid, the original owner has 2 options.
They may retain that player’s rights for 1 year at the Qualifying offer price.  Player will become a free-agent the following season.
They may cut the player prior to the Free-Agent  Draft with no Cap Penalty.
If at the end of the franchise period, the qualifying offer is outbid, the original owner has 2 options:
Match the highest offer made by the end of the day on June 17th.  This will allow owner to retain player for 1 year at the matched Salary
Allow the player to “walk” and receive compensation from the team that had the highest bid.
Compensation for a Transition Tagged Player is a 2nd and a 3rd round pick (Must be from current season cannot be deferred)

In the event the owner who won the bid on the Transition Player has two (2) 2nd round picks or two (2) 3rd round picks in the upcoming draft, the team that let the player “walk” can decide what picks they want to receive if the player that they let “walk” ended the prior season in the Top 10 in Total Points for their respective position.

In other words, Team A lets a player “walk” who was the 10th highest rated player (or better) in his position the previous season, to Team B who has the 1st pick in the 2nd as well as the 3rd pick in the 2nd.  In this scenario, Team A can choose the earlier pick.
If Team A lets a player “walk” who was ranked 11th or lower, Team B decides what picks he will relinquish.
If player is allowed to walk, the team that has won the “Transition Tagged” player has the right to offer said a player a multiple year contract prior to Free-Agent  Draft.
Unless a team has the required compensation necessary to compensate an owner, they will be prohibited from placing any such bid on a “Franchise Tagged Player”
Franchise and transition players may not be traded during this franchise and transition tag bidding period.
Only teams with the required draft choice compensation are eligible for bidding on franchise and transition players.
FRONT AND BACK LOADED CONTRACTS: Maximum allowed on a roster at any point is 5
Can be applied to Contract Extensions and to Free Agent  Draft Contracts
Does not apply to Rookie Contracts
Example:  Win Arian Foster in Free Agent  Draft for $65K (Roughly Highest Salary in 2011)
Year 2 45K (130K* 35%) = $45.5K Take Total Salary $130 less amount Paid ($85K) = $45K (Not rounded up)
Example:  Win Arian Foster in Free Agent  Draft for $65K (Roughly Highest Salary in 2011)
Example:  Win Arian Foster in Free Agent  Draft for $65K (Roughly Highest Salary in 2011)
Year 2 84K (130K* 65%) = $84.5K Take Total Salary $130 less amount Paid ($46K) = $84K (Not rounded up)
Example:  Win Arian Foster in Free Agent  Draft for $65K (Roughly Highest Salary in 2011)
WEEKLY TRANSACTION PERIOD: A weekly transaction period includes Waiver Wire Claims, followed by Bidding on Free Agents.
Waiver wire claims will be processed (Tuesday Mornings at 7 AM).
Free agent blind bids will be processed (Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday and Sunday at 9AM).
Sundays at 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM players may be picked up as free agents as first come first serve
The “Weekly Transaction Period” will begin on the first Wednesday following the completion  draft each year and will end at 1 PM on Week 16.
WAIVERS TRANSACTIONS: Any player waived by a team is available on the league’s waiver wire the following transaction period.  The deadline for waiver transactions will be Tuesday Mornings prior to 7 AM.
A player’s current contract, salary and year of contract remain intact when the player is claimed off the waiver wire by another team.

If a player goes unclaimed through the waiver wire process, they become an unrestricted free agent and they are eligible for bidding in the current transaction period, following the week of being cut.
Once a waiver claim is made by a team owner it may not be revoked.
Team owners may claim one player from waivers each week after which they go to the end of the line pending claims by other team owners in the case of multiple claims by one team.
If no other team claims the subsequent player(s), then the owner may claim a second player from waivers, and so on.
League will use a “rolling” waiver order, that resets each year based on the draft order.
BLIND BID FREE AGENCY: Blind Bid Waivers are run Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday & Sunday Mornings at 9 AM.
Owners may submit bids on players who are not on rosters and who have cleared waivers (Free-Agents).
Owners may bid up to their cap on a player.
Winning bid on a player becomes their contract amount.
Free-Agents are automatically signed to a 1 yr contract.
There is a 2 week window from the time a free-agent is acquired to “negotiate” an extension. See Chapter 6.04
If multiple teams bid on the same player, the player is awarded to the team with the highest bid.
If multiple teams bid the same amount on the same player, the player will be rewarded to the using the following tie breaker scenario on a worst to first basis.
If multiple teams bid the same amount on a player prior to Week 1, the player will be awarded to the team who finished lower in the league standings the previous season.
FIRST COME FIRST SERVE FREE AGENCY: First Come First Serve Free Agency (FCFSFA) runs Sunday Mornings at 9:00 AM until 1:00 PM.
Owners may freely pick up players who have not been selected via Blind Bid Waivers on a first come first served basis.
Players picked up via FCFSFA are signed to a1 yr $1,000 contract.
There is a 2 week window from the time a free-agent is acquired to “negotiate” an extension. See Chapter
CUTTING PLAYERS: Any player cut whose TOTAL SALARY is GREATER than $4000 will be subject to a 20% cap penalty based on TOTAL SALARY REMAINING.
Total Salary = Salary due to player.   For a normal contract this is simply Contract Years remaining on Contract * Salary per year.
For Special Front or Back Loaded Contracts this is the total Salary still owed to player.
Penalties accumulate over the course of the season and off-season up until the Free-Agent  Draft
The Cap Penalty will be applied at the start of the off-season.
The Cap Penalty will be rounded either up or down to the nearest whole thousand prior to Free- Agent  Draft.
Any player cut after the start of the Free Agent  Draft who is subject to a cap penalty will be applied to the following season’s Cap.
Tom Brady signs a 2 year $50K contract and is cut during season 1.  He will be subject to a 20% hit on 2 years @ 50K or $20K hit.
If Tom is cut after February 1st, his contract will have rolled forward and now is only owed 1 year @ $50K or a $10K hit
Any player cut during the off-season with remaining years left on contract is NOT ELIGIBLE to be bid on by the owner who cut said player during the Free-Agent  Draft.
This is to enforce the importance of making sound decisions with the players that you sign.
SPECIAL CIRCUMSTANCE (HOLD-OUTS): If a player on a roster is holding out as of September 1st, that player will receive a 25% Cap Relief.
Once player returns from hold-out relief is removed.
If player is still holding out as of Week 8, the owner have 2 options
If owner wants contract to remain as is and roll-forward at end of year.
CAP Relief remains at 25% until player returns to NFL
Player takes up a roster spot
Contract rolls 1 year forward in February
If owner would like players’ contract to freeze and not count as a year.
A Roster Spot is Cleared up.
Player gets dropped to “Limbo” where he will be locked for the remainder of year and CC will place back on roster after Contracts Roll Forward
Player is NOT ELIGIBLE to be used the remainder of this season.
Contract years do not change the following season.
SUSPENSIONS, RETIRES, JAIL BIRDS: Any player who gets suspended for an entire/remainder of season, retires, or goes to jail while locked up under contract may be cut with no cap penalty.
Owner relinquishes rights to this player.
Owner may retain these players if they like with a 50% Cap Relief.
Player still takes up a roster spot
Cap Relief goes away once player returns to NFL.
Contract rolls forward if incident occurred in-season.
Contract does not roll-forward if incident occurred in off-season.

This only applies if the player was under contract prior to one of these special circumstances.  If A.Foster goes to Jail for a year, and is a free-agent, you will not receive the relief noted in this section once you sign him to a deal.
TRADES: Our league uses the MFL system to enter, negotiate and process trades. Trades shall be subject to a 2 day voting period.  Once the trade receives 7 votes in favor, it can be processed by a member of the Competition Committee.
Trading may take place during the trading periods as dictated by the League Calendar.

If the trade reaches (6) votes against it, all owners who voted against the trade will be required to post in the message board topic that a member of the Competition Committee creates indicating why they feel the trade hurts the integrity of the league. (Not if they feel a trade is one-sided).  This must be done within 24 hours of the message

If an owner fails to respond to this message board topic within the 24 hour time frame, their vote will be changed from a vote against the trade to a vote that is for the trade thereby allowing the trade to be pushed through as approved.
Teams may trade future draft choices up to one year following the current season.
In the Off-Season (Once the upcoming season’s site has been setup owners may trade picks that includes picks for the next draft.

Any attempt to dump players, stack teams, or conduct trades otherwise viewed as one-sided, unethical or involving the exchange of money are strictly illegal and are subject to a league vote for possible removal from the league.
Such trades may be vetoed immediately by the CC.
Team owners guilty of said offenses are subject to a fine, or even the loss of their franchise immediately pending a vote of all league members not involved in the trade.

Trades involving teams eliminated from the playoff contention are subject to the scrutiny of the other league members and ultimately may need the approval of a league vote before being accepted as binding.
LEAGUE SANCTIONS AND FINES: If during the Free-Agent  Draft a team bids on a player and it puts them over the salary cap or if they don’t have enough money remaining to fulfill the minimum roster requirements using the minimum bid for remaining open roster spots, they will be subject to a $10 fine due with next season’s league dues.

Conducting unethical trades or moves in a way that jeopardizes the integrity of the league will be subject to discipline.  The CC will convene and determine a sanction that is in line with action that was committed.  Sanction could result in removal from the league.

All fines collected will be used to help off-set the cost of the league site the following year, or if more has been collected, this will be distributed in accordance to how Competition Committee rules for the following season.
